title: Calonema aestivum
intro: Calonema aestivum, also known as Atractylis gummifera or Atractylodes, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, offering several health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and digestive support properties. The bioactive constituents of Calonema aestivum include sesquiterpene lactones, particularly atractyloside, as well as other compounds like atractylodin, atractylenolide, and sesquiterpene glycosides. In traditional herbal medicine, Calonema aestivum is often prepared into dried roots, extracts, or decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Calonema aestivum belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. The genus Calonema is a small group of orchids native to Asia, particularly in China and Southeast Asia. Calonema aestivum is one of the species within this genus. Its classification as an orchid places it among the third-largest family of flowering plants.
uses: Calonema aestivum, although limited information is available on this species, it is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of various infections. Additionally, Calonema aestivum may have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate pain and swelling in the body. Its supposed medicinal uses also include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as colds and coughs.
